core_claim: HexStrike AI v6.0 connects AI agents (Claude, Cursor, Copilot) to 127 security tools plus the BOAZ EDR evasion framework via MCP, turning prompt-driven red team ops into a click.

slides.on_slide_copy: HexStrike AI v6.0 bridges LLMs to 127 security tools via MCP — nmap, nuclei, sqlmap, bloodhound, plus BOAZ's 77 process injection loaders and 12 encoding schemes.

slides.on_slide_copy: One prompt can now chain recon → vuln scan → exploitation → EDR-evading payload delivery. Attack velocity compresses from days to minutes. BOAZ's Pluto/Akira LLVM obfuscation and 77 loaders bypass signature, heuristic, and behavioral detection.
slides.subline: Lowers the skill floor for sophisticated red team tradecraft.

slides.on_slide_copy: FastMCP server exposes 127 tools + BOAZ as MCP functions. AI client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code Copilot, Roo Code) calls tools via standardized protocol. Decision engine selects tools, optimizes params, handles chaining. BOAZ pipeline: msfvenom payload → entropy analysis → evasion layers (encoding, obfuscation, anti-analysis) → stealth binary.
slides.subline: MCP standardizes the LLM ↔ tool contract; BOAZ wraps the output.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: abstract flow diagram rendered as glowing nodes — LLM agent at center connecting to tool clusters (recon, web, binary, cloud) which feed into an evasion engine block, dark cybersecurity aesthetic, no readable code or exploit detail

slides.on_slide_copy: Static signatures miss BOAZ's polymorphic output. Behavioral rules don't catch threadless injection or VEH/VCH execution guards. Most EDRs still rely on API hooks that Halo's Gate and syscall stubs bypass. Detection assumes human-paced tool chains — not AI-orchestrated parallel execution.
slides.subline: Defenses built for manual red teams don't match AI velocity.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: a SOC analyst overwhelmed by a flood of alerts — traditional detection rules lighting up individually while a coordinated AI-driven attack flows through the gaps, dark high-contrast, tension in the composition

slides.on_slide_copy: Deploy API monitoring on MCP servers — log every tool call with agent identity. Hunt for BOAZ artifacts: entropy anomalies, LLVM-obfuscated binaries, known loader stubs (Ninja, Sifu, Halo's Gate). Enforce LLM tool-use policies: approval gates for payload generation, rate limits on tool chains. Test your EDR against BOAZ loaders in a lab — if it catches them, you have a baseline.
slides.subline: Treat MCP servers like any privileged automation — audit, alert, restrict.

caption:
AI agents just got the keys to 127 security tools and an EDR evasion framework.

HexStrike AI v6.0 connects Claude, Cursor, Copilot, and Roo Code to nmap, nuclei, sqlmap, bloodhound — plus BOAZ's 77 process injection loaders, 12 encoding schemes, and Pluto/Akira LLVM obfuscation — all via MCP. One prompt now chains recon → vuln scan → exploitation → stealth payload delivery.

The skill floor for sophisticated red team tradecraft just collapsed. Static signatures miss polymorphic BOAZ output. Behavioral rules don't catch threadless injection or VEH/VCH execution guards. Halo's Gate and syscall stubs bypass the API hooks most EDRs still rely on.

Defensive move: deploy API monitoring on your MCP servers — log every tool call with agent identity. Hunt for entropy anomalies and known loader stubs (Ninja, Sifu, Halo's Gate). Enforce LLM tool-use policies with approval gates for payload generation. Test your EDR against BOAZ loaders in a lab — if it catches them, you have a baseline.

How are you testing your detection stack against AI-orchestrated tool chains?

Follow for AI security breakdowns without the fake panic.

narration.text: AI agents just got the keys to 127 security tools and an EDR-evasion framework.
HexStrike AI v6.0 connects Claude, Cursor, and Copilot to nmap, nuclei, and sqlmap, plus BOAZ's loaders and encoding schemes, all through MCP.
Deploy API monitoring on your MCP servers. Hunt for BOAZ artifacts like entropy anomalies and known loader stubs, and test your EDR against BOAZ in a lab.
AI-driven red teaming isn't coming. It's here now, and one prompt replaces days of manual tradecraft.
How are you testing your detection stack against AI-orchestrated tool chains? Comment and save.
